top_headlines: From Classic To Contemporary: 12 Diwali 2025 Outfit Ideas For Every Celebration
Where To Buy Traditional Indian Clothes In Singapore 2026

bet_override: Override: demand signals are weak despite active editorial/social buzz. Social buzz may not convert to value-fashion sales. Watch 4 weeks before any commitment.

india_fit.price_band_reason: Sharara kurti sets are predominantly positioned in the ₹399–₹1,299 bracket at Meesho/Amazon, aligning with your value-fashion ceiling.
india_fit.climate_fit_reason: Sharara sets work for festive/cooler seasons and air-conditioned indoor events, but the layered silhouette and dupatta may feel heavy during summer and monsoon in most Indian climates.
india_fit.occasion_fit: festive, ethnic, wedding-adjacent, Diwali, family celebrations
india_fit.cultural_fit_reason: Sharara sets carry strong cultural legitimacy in North and Central India, with modest proportions and traditional aesthetics widely accepted across conservative and urban audiences.
india_fit.value_fashion_fit_reason: Meesho's 10 active listings and discount signals confirm customer willingness to transact; Tier 2–3 festive demand aligns with sharara's occasion-driven nature.

claude_synthesis.convergence_summary: Signals show a moderate, occasion-specific trend with seasonal demand spikes (Diwali 2025) rather than sustained year-round momentum; editorial coverage amplifies visibility, but marketplace depth and search interest remain modest.
claude_synthesis.signal_agreement: Partial disagreement: Google Trends shows 71% lift over six weeks (strong), but Myntra/Meesho catalog presence is thin (10 listings) and marketplace discounts suggest inventory pressure, not organic pull.
claude_synthesis.disagreement_note: Resolve via: (1) Check Meesho/Myntra sales velocity and return rates on existing sharara kurti listings; (2) Track Google Trends weekly through Diwali 2025 to confirm sustained lift vs. one-time spike; (3) Survey Tier 2 retailer sell-through data if you stock test units.
claude_synthesis.bet_reasoning: Google Trends lift (71% in 6 weeks) + seasonal editorial buzz (Diwali 2025 guides) + cultural legitimacy suggest a real festive micro-trend worth a controlled buy-in of 300–500 units across Tier 2–3 geographies. However, flat absolute search interest (12/100 vs. 100 peak) and shallow marketplace competition indicate this is not a mainstream breakout—position it as a seasonal festive offer, not core assortment.
claude_synthesis.skepticism_flag: Google Trends lift may reflect Diwali seasonal noise and organic search interest from legacy searches rather than intent to purchase at value-fashion price points; the 90-day window captures pre-festive planning, which inflates perceived demand that may not sustain post-Diwali.
